title: Ensure Automatic Error Reporting is not enabled

prose.en: Ensure Automatic Error Reporting is not enabled

description.en: The Apport Error Reporting Service automatically generates crash reports for debugging

rationale.en: Apport collects potentially sensitive data, such as core dumps, stack traces, and log files. They can contain passwords, credit card numbers, serial numbers, and other private material.

audit.en:
Edit `/etc/default/apport` and add or edit the enabled parameter to equal `0`:  

Run the following commands to stop and mask the apport service  

**\- OR \-**  

Run the following command to remove the apport package:

```
# apt purge apport
```

```
# systemctl stop apport.service
# systemctl mask apport.service
```

```
enabled=0
```

remediation.en:
Edit `/etc/default/apport` and add or edit the enabled parameter to equal `0`:  

Run the following commands to stop and mask the apport service  

**\- OR \-**  

Run the following command to remove the apport package:

```
# apt purge apport
```

```
# systemctl stop apport.service
# systemctl mask apport.service
```

```
enabled=0
```
